title: System intent satisfaction: total completion cost
scope: Governing intent: Reduce total successful-completion cost across restart, handoff, review, and proof loops.
goal: Assess whether package behavior optimizes total successful-completion cost rather than local step cost.
non_goals: Do not run a fresh benchmark.
Do not treat token count as the only cost signal.

review_mode.review question: Does current workflow guidance make total completion cost visible enough to influence decisions?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: README thesis, startup delegation decision, capability-aware execution docs, maintainer dogfooding guidance

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json; rg cost/token/overhead/proof/handoff across README and docs

findings.title: Satisfaction is medium-high, but evidence remains more qualitative than measured
findings.summary: The product thesis now honestly frames overhead as an investment, and startup/delegation output can avoid unnecessary planning or delegation. The remaining weakness is that cost improvement is still mostly judged through dogfooding and evaluation notes rather than a compact recurring report.
findings.evidence: README says the package adds small intentional coordination overhead; startup chose stay-local with token_savings_expected none for this task; capability docs say delegation should happen only when handoff costs less than rediscovery.
findings.risk if unchanged: Without periodic measured or ledger-backed cost evidence, the package may keep optimizing perceived ergonomics while missing total successful-completion regressions.
findings.suggested action: Defer until the next evaluation lane; if cost claims become central again, prefer a compact evaluation ledger summary over new prose doctrine.
findings.promotion trigger: A future optimization loop lacks enough cost evidence to decide whether a workflow surface should stay, shrink, or move behind an opt-in profile.

recommendation.defer: Use next evaluation work to strengthen cost evidence if decisions need it.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not add measurement machinery solely from this review.

retention.trigger: superseded by a focused evaluation-ledger or cost report review
retention.proof surface: evaluation ledger, startup output, and capability-aware execution docs

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
